title: Adding Plants
description: How to add plants, set care thresholds, and personalize your garden.

sections.body: Each plant in Plantgotchi gets its own profile with custom care thresholds. Here's how to set one up.

sections.heading: Creating a Plant Profile
sections.body:
1. Open your Garden dashboard and tap the "Add Plant" button.
  2. Give your plant a name — get creative! "Fernando the Fern" works great.
  3. Optionally set a species (e.g., "Monstera Deliciosa") for more targeted AI recommendations.
  4. Pick an emoji avatar from the selector — this appears on your plant card.
  5. Optionally add a photo URL if you want a real picture of your plant.

sections.heading: Setting Thresholds
sections.body:
Thresholds tell Plantgotchi when to alert you. You can customize these for each plant:

MetricDefault MinDefault MaxNotes
Moisture30%80%Good for most houseplants
Temperature15°C30°CTropical plants may need higher

You can also set a light preference — low, medium, or high. This is used by the rule engine: if your plant prefers high light but the sensor reads below 1,000 lux, you'll get a gentle reminder to move it.

sections.heading: Light Preference Guide
sections.body:
• Low — under 1,000 lux (e.g., snake plant, pothos, ZZ plant)
  • Medium — 1,000–2,000 lux (e.g., philodendron, peace lily)
  • High — over 2,000 lux (e.g., succulents, herbs, fiddle leaf fig)

sections.heading: Editing and Deleting Plants
sections.body: Tap on any plant card to open its detail view, then use the edit button to change its name, thresholds, or emoji. You can also delete a plant — this removes the plant profile but preserves historical sensor readings in the database.
